title: Activated charcoal melt-and-pour soap
subtitle: A deep-cleansing black bar with activated charcoal, tea tree, and peppermint
excerpt: Activated charcoal turns the bar a striking dark grey to black and is mildly adsorptive at the surface, making it useful for oily and blemish-prone skin. Tea tree and peppermint essential oils add a clean, fresh scent. Melt-and-pour base means no lye handling.
sourceNotes: Activated charcoal usage rate in M&P soap (1-2% of base weight): standard cosmetic craft reference. Tea tree and peppermint EO in soap for oily-skin formulations: cosmetic craft practice. Clear soap base recommended with charcoal for deepest black colour.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Keep activated charcoal at 1% of base weight -- too much produces a chalky bar that transfers black residue to towels.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: No cure required. Bars are ready to use once fully set.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Keep for one year wrapped in clingfilm or boxed. Activated charcoal will dust if the bar surface chips -- wrap individually to prevent mess.

glossaryTerms.definition: Fine black cosmetic-grade powder made by heating carbon-rich materials at high temperatures. Used as a natural colourant and mild adsorptive ingredient in cleansing products. For cosmetic use only -- not for internal use in this context.

content.text: produces a deep charcoal-grey bar. White base works too but gives a softer grey. The charcoal quantity here is deliberately modest -- 1% of base weight (5 g) is enough to colour the bar thoroughly without producing a bar that smears dark residue on towels. Pre-mix the charcoal with a little melted base to eliminate lumps before adding to the main batch.

items.prepNote: 1% of base weight; pre-disperse in a little melted base

content.text: Cut the soap base into cubes and melt over a double boiler or in the microwave in 30-second bursts. Do not overheat. Cool to about 60°C.

Weigh the activated charcoal into a small container. Add one tablespoon of the melted base and stir to a smooth, lump-free paste. Add this paste back to the main batch and stir thoroughly. Add the tea tree and peppermint essential oils and stir gently. The batch will be a uniform dark grey at this point.

Pour steadily into the silicone loaf mould. Mist the surface with isopropyl alcohol to eliminate bubbles. Leave to set at room temperature for at least two hours. Unmould and slice into 6 to 8 bars. Note that the cut surfaces may show a lighter interior -- this is normal and the colour evens with use.

Activated charcoal soap rinses clean without leaving residue on skin at 1% usage. It is particularly well suited to oily, combination, and blemish-prone skin as a facial or body bar. Avoid using on very dry or sensitive skin.
